title: (No AI) Microsoft’s Copilot AI assistant is coming to current-gen Xbox consoles this year
author: Emma Roth
content:
![The white Xbox Series X](https://platform.theverge.com/wp-content/uploads/sites/2/chorus/uploads/chorus_asset/file/25676324/twarren_xboxseriesxwhite__6_.JPG?quality=90&strip=all&crop=0,0,100,100)

Xbox is getting ready to launch its Gaming Copilot AI assistant on "current-generation consoles" this year, according to [a report from _GamesRadar_](https://www.gamesradar.com/games/xbox-just-revealed-gaming-copilot-is-coming-to-current-generation-consoles-later-this-year/). Sonali Yadav, Xbox's product manager for gaming AI, revealed the news during a panel at the Game Developers Conference (GDC), adding that the company will also add the assistant to "more services that players are playing."

Microsoft has been working on its [gaming-focused Copilot assistant](https://www.theverge.com/news/628666/microsoft-xbox-copilot-for-gaming) for months now, with the company launching it in beta on the Xbox mobile app, [Windows 11](https://www.theverge.com/news/719668/microsoft-xbox-gaming-copilot-windows-game-bar-beta-test), and [Xbox Ally handhelds](https://www.theverge.com/news/682011/microsoft-windows-xbox-pc-combination-features-rog-xbox-ally-devices). Players can use their voice to call upon the Gaming Copilot if they get stuck in a game, and th …

[Read the full story at The Verge.](https://www.theverge.com/games/894799/microsoft-gaming-copilot-ai-xbox-consoles)
contentSnippet:
Xbox is getting ready to launch its Gaming Copilot AI assistant on "current-generation consoles" this year, according to a report from GamesRadar. Sonali Yadav, Xbox's product manager for gaming AI, revealed the news during a panel at the Game Developers Conference (GDC), adding that the company will also add the assistant to "more services that players are playing." 
Microsoft has been working on its gaming-focused Copilot assistant for months now, with the company launching it in beta on the Xbox mobile app, Windows 11, and Xbox Ally handhelds. Players can use their voice to call upon the Gaming Copilot if they get stuck in a game, and th …
Read the full story at The Verge.

title: (No AI) Facebook Marketplace adds AI auto-replies for annoying ‘Is this still available?’ messages
author: Emma Roth
content:
![An image of the Facebook logo surrounded by blue circles](https://platform.theverge.com/wp-content/uploads/sites/2/2025/04/STK040_VRG_Illo_N_Barclay_2_facebook.jpg?quality=90&strip=all&crop=0,0,100,100)

Facebook Marketplace is [adding a bunch of new AI-powered tools](https://about.fb.com/news/2026/03/facebook-marketplace-new-meta-ai-tools-make-selling-faster-and-easier/) that are supposed to make selling items on the platform a little more efficient. One feature will use Meta AI to automatically respond to those annoying "Is this still available?" messages.

You can toggle on the auto-reply option when creating a listing, and Meta AI will draft an editable response to any questions related to availability. In an example shown by Meta, its AI assistant creates an auto-reply saying, "Yes, it's still available. Do you have any questions?"

![](https://platform.theverge.com/wp-content/uploads/sites/2/2026/03/auto-reply-fb.webp?quality=90&strip=all&crop=0,0,100,100)

Facebook Marketplace is trying to speed up the process of listing items, too, as Meta AI can now use the photos …

[Read the full story at The Verge.](https://www.theverge.com/tech/893907/facebook-marketplace-ai-auto-reply-listings)

You can toggle on the auto-reply option when creating a listing, and Meta AI will draft an editable response to any questions related to availability. In an example shown by Meta, its AI assistant creates an auto-reply saying, "Yes, it's still available. Do you have any questions?"
Facebook Marketplace is trying to speed up the process of listing items, too, as Meta AI can now use the photos …
Read the full story at The Verge.

creator: Zeyi Yang
title: (No AI) China’s OpenClaw Boom Is a Gold Rush for AI Companies
dc:creator: Zeyi Yang
content: Hype around the open source agent is driving people to rent cloud servers and buy AI subscriptions just to try it, creating a windfall for tech companies.
